---
title: "Publications and Presentations"
description-meta: "Information, pdfs, and social metrics of papers"
#page-layout: full
back-to-top-navigation: true
title-block-banner: true
css: custom.css
---
<!-- ## Selected Publications -->

## Working Papers and Manuscripts
*Since these works are currently in preparation or under review, please contact me for the latest drafts.*

- **Neural Dynamic Portfolio Control with Provable Learning Guarantees** | Yizhe Huang, [Rui Gao](https://rgao32.github.io/index.html), [Shuang Li](https://shuangli01.github.io/index.html), [Luhao Zhang](https://luhao-zhang.github.io/) (*Revision* at Management Science)  <br>
<div class="text-muted" style="font-size: 0.9em;"> Are there neural approaches to portfolio control that incorporate historical returns with provable end-to-end global guarantees?
<a href="/papers/2025/neural-portfolio-control.qmd" class="float-end">See more... »</a>
</div>


- **Nonsmooth Riemannian Optimization with Inexact Information** | [Mateo Díaz](https://mateodd25.github.io/) and [Benjamin Grimmer](https://www.ams.jhu.edu/~grimmer/) (Submitted) <br>
<div class="text-muted" style="font-size: 0.9em;"> Can nonsmooth convex Riemannian optimization admit nonasymptotic convergence rates using only subgradients, first-order retractions, and vector transports?
<a href="/papers/2026/riemannian-bundle-method.qmd" class="float-end">See more... »</a>
</div>


- **Constrained Fréchet Means of Unknown Submanifolds of Non-Euclidean Spaces** | [Mauro Maggioni](https://mauromaggioni.duckdns.org/) (In-Preparation) <br>
<div class="text-muted" style="font-size: 0.9em;"> Given only a local sampler of a non-convex submanifold of some non-Euclidean space, is there an algorithm to compute the empirical intrinsic mean up to arbitrary accuracy with provable guarantees?
<!-- <a href="/papers/2026/wasserstein-barycenter.qmd" class="float-end">See more... »</a> -->
</div>

<!-- ## Publications

### Peer-Reviewed Journal Articles

### Conference Proceedings -->

## Talks

**Nonsmooth Riemannian Optimization with Inexact Manifold Primitives via Bundle Methods**

*   **SIAM Conference on Optimization (OP26)**, University of Edinburgh | June 2026
*   **Modeling and Optimization: Theory and Applications (MOPTA)**, Lehigh University | August 2026

**Constrained Fréchet Means of Unknown Submanifolds of Non-Euclidean Spaces** 

*   **INFORMS Annual Meeting** *(Session Organizer)* — Session: *Optimization in Data Science* (co-organized with [Mateo Díaz](https://mateodd25.github.io/)), San Francisco | November 2026
*   **SIAM Conference on Mathematics of Data Science (MDS26)** *(Session Organizer)* — Session: *Geometry, Dynamics, and Inference* (co-organized with [Mauro Maggioni](https://mauromaggioni.duckdns.org/) and [George Kevrekidis](https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=rynRUuQAAAAJ&view_op=list_works&sortby=pubdate)) | November 2026

**Convergence Rates for Riemannian Proximal Bundle Methods**

*   **International Conference on Continuous Optimization (ICCOPT)**, USC | July 2025
*   **Jr. Mathematical Institute of Data Science (MINDS) Seminar**, Johns Hopkins | April 2025
*   **SIAM Student Seminar**, University of Wisconsin-Madison | April 2025
*   **Applied Mathematics and Statistics Student Seminar**, Johns Hopkins | March 2025

**Empirical Wasserstein Barycenters: Riemannian Optimization Guarantees**

*   **Applied Mathematics and Statistics Student Seminar**, Johns Hopkins | October 2024
*   **Applied Mathematics and Statistics Student Seminar**, Johns Hopkins | January 2024

## Posters

**Computing Empirical Barycenters on Unknown Wasserstein Submanifolds** [Poster](/posts.qmd)  
*Presented at the following venues:* 

*   **Mathematical Aspects of Data Science Summer School**, EFPL | Sept 2025
*   **Princeton Machine Learning Theory Summer School** | Aug 2025
*   **SIAM Conference on Applied Mathematics**, Johns Hopkins | April 2025
*   **Statistics and Optimal Transport Workshop**, Columbia University | March 2025
*   **Optimization Workshop**, Universidad de los Andes | Dec 2024

<!--Include academic icons or buttons-->
{{< include /files/includes/_academic.qmd >}}

<!-- Notes: \* corresponding author; <sup>GS</sup> graduate student advisee; <sup>VS</sup> visiting scholar; ![](https://drganghe.github.io/files/images/highly-cited-paper-badge.png "ESI Highly Cited Paper"){width=20 fig-alt="Highly cited paper"} ESI [Highly Cited Paper](https://webofscience.help.clarivate.com/en-us/Content/esi-highly-cited-papers.html) (top 1%); ![](https://drganghe.github.io/files/images/hot-paper-badge.png "ESI Hot Paper"){width=20 fig-alt="Hot paper"} ESI [Hot Paper](https://webofscience.help.clarivate.com/en-us/Content/esi-hot-papers.html) (top 0.1%)  -->


<!-- ## Selected Work -->

<!-- Einstein, A., Podolsky, B., & Rosen, N. (1935). [Can quantum-mechanical description of physical reality be considered complete?](posts/paper-title/index.qmd). *Physical review*, 47(10), 777. \[[pdf](https://scholar.google.com/)\] \[[code and data](https://doi.org)\] ![](https://drganghe.github.io/files/images/highly-cited-paper-badge.png "ESI Highly Cited Paper"){width=20 fig-alt="Highly cited paper"} ![](https://drganghe.github.io/files/images/hot-paper-badge.png "ESI Hot Paper"){width=20 fig-alt="Hot paper"} <span class="__dimensions_badge_embed__" data-doi="10.1103/PhysRev.47.777" data-style="large_rectangle"></span> <div data-badge-popover="right" data-badge-type="1" data-doi="10.1103/PhysRev.47.777" data-hide-no-mentions="true" class="altmetric-embed"></div>      -->

<!-- This object here makes nice spacing for selected works -->
<!-- <center><hr style="width:50%"></center> -->

<!-- Einstein, A. (1965). Concerning an heuristic point of view toward the emission and transformation of light. *American Journal of Physics*, 33(5), 367. [[pdf](https://icg.isy.liu.se/en/phd-courses/quantum_foundations/references/Einstein1905b.pdf)] <span class="__dimensions_badge_embed__" data-doi="10.1038/s41560-023-01304-w" data-style="large_rectangle"></span> <div data-badge-popover="right" data-badge-type="1" data-doi="10.1038/s41560-023-01304-w" data-hide-no-mentions="true" class="altmetric-embed"></div> -->



<!-- Include social share buttons

{{< include /files/includes/_socialshare.qmd >}} -->
